records.ety: [Pref. im- not + prescriptible: cf. F. imprescriptible.]

defs: Not capable of being lost or impaired by neglect, by disuse, or by the claims of another founded on prescription; -- of rights.
quotes: The right of navigation, fishing, and others that may be exercised on the sea, belonging to the right of mere ability, are imprescriptible.

defs: Not derived from, or dependent on, external authority; self-evidencing; obvious.
quotes: The imprescriptible laws of the pure reason.
